$comment: Manifest mapping profiles to skills/hooks/templates/agents, consumed by install.ps1 (--Profile) / install.sh (--profile). 'skills'/'hooks'/'templates'/'agents' are SHIPPED — they MUST exist on disk; the installer treats a missing shipped item as a hard error (profiles.json out of sync with the repo, not a planned gap). 'plannedSkills'/'plannedHooks'/'plannedTemplates'/'plannedAgents' are declared for roadmap visibility only, may legitimately not exist yet, and are reported as 'planned, not installed' without error. 'agents'/'plannedAgents' are optional per profile — a profile without them ships no agents (backward compatible with pre-0.4.0 copies).

defaults.note: If no --profile/-Profile is passed, the installer installs core + java-spring-backend.

core.description: Stack-agnostic SDD lifecycle, guardrails, and generic reviews. Always installed.

java-spring-backend.description: Java 17/21, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, Maven (primary), REST APIs, JPA/Hibernate, Flyway, Keycloak/OAuth2, Vault. Default profile.
java-spring-backend.note: Phase 2 delivered 4 review skills, 2 defensive hooks (maven-compile shipped earlier), and 3 context templates. Phase 5 DEPRECATED maven-compile in favor of java-build-test-guard (a strict superset); maven-compile stays in this hooks list so existing wirings keep installing, but the settings templates now wire java-build-test-guard and the two must never be wired together (double compile). Phase 3 folded contract-testing-reviewer into microservices-patterns-reviewer's Contract compatibility section (shipped under the messaging-event-driven profile, not here) instead of shipping it as a separate skill here — see specs/features/003-phase3-event-driven-microservices/DECISIONS.md D002. CONTRACT_TESTING.md was dropped from plannedTemplates for the same reason (see D006); its content lives in docs/_templates/MICROSERVICES_PATTERNS.md's Contract testing setup section. observability-reviewer and OBSERVABILITY.md remain planned, deferred to a future phase.

messaging-event-driven.description: Kafka, RabbitMQ, ActiveMQ, event-driven architecture, outbox, saga, exactly-once/at-least-once semantics, microservices architectural patterns and cross-service contract compatibility.
messaging-event-driven.note: Phase 3 shipped 2 review skills (event-driven-reviewer, microservices-patterns-reviewer) and 2 context templates. kafka-reviewer, rabbitmq-reviewer, outbox-pattern-reviewer, and saga-orchestration-reviewer were retired as separate roadmap items — their scope is fully absorbed into event-driven-reviewer's sections (see DECISIONS D001). contract-testing-reviewer was never shipped here either — its scope is a section inside microservices-patterns-reviewer (see D002). messaging-review-reminder hook remains planned. This is an OPTIONAL profile, conceptually layered on java-spring-backend (its skills assume a Java/Spring service underneath) but NOT an automatic overlay: '--profile messaging-event-driven' installs core + messaging-event-driven ONLY. To also install java-spring-backend, list both explicitly: '--profile java-spring-backend,messaging-event-driven' (install.sh) or '-Profile java-spring-backend,messaging-event-driven' (install.ps1) — see DECISIONS D003.

payments-fintech.description: Payment processing, Stripe/PSP integration, idempotency, reconciliation, money/units safety, webhook signature verification.
payments-fintech.note: Overlay profile. Can combine with java-spring-backend or next-prisma-web. Nothing is shipped yet.

next-prisma-web.description: Next.js App Router, React, TypeScript, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, Server Components/Actions, Tailwind.
next-prisma-web.note: Optional profile. The 3 shipped skills and 3 hooks are generic/stack-agnostic already present in the repo. seo-review moved out to the seo-geo-addon overlay profile (SEO/GEO/AEO are separately billed services — see specs/features/008-billable-service-scope). prisma-migration-reviewer, nextjs-server-actions-reviewer, and prisma-migration-guard are planned.

seo-geo-addon.description: Technical SEO, GEO (generative engine optimization), AEO (answer engine optimization), and AI-visibility review. Billable add-on — install only when the client has contracted seo/geo/aeo/ai-visibility.
seo-geo-addon.note: Overlay profile, not an automatic addition to next-prisma-web. Currently ships seo-review only (technical on-page SEO + hreflang); GEO, AEO, and AI-visibility are declared as plannedSkills so the profile honestly reflects that they are three separate future reviewers, not covered by seo-review today (see specs/features/008-billable-service-scope DECISIONS D006). Install alongside the web profile with the client's contracted services declared in specs/SERVICES.md: '--profile next-prisma-web,seo-geo-addon' (install.sh) or '-Profile next-prisma-web,seo-geo-addon' (install.ps1).

blockchain-crypto.note: Placeholder. Nothing shipped or planned yet. disabled=true is enforced by the installer: requesting this profile explicitly via --profile/-Profile fails with a clear error rather than silently installing nothing. There is no flag to force-enable it — that requires an explicit code change plus a new spec.
